key_insight: A PFEM mesh adaptation strategy preserves sharp multiphase interfaces by forcing interface edges into the Delaunay triangulation, reducing mesh-driven topology errors.
engineering_relevance: This decouples phase-change and interface evolution from user mesh resolution, enabling more reliable multiphase simulation with fewer nodes and better geometric fidelity.
method: Fully Lagrangian PFEM for arbitrary immiscible phases
Assign a node-empty disk to each interface segment to guarantee Delaunay edge inclusion
Use dynamic mesh adaptation instead of constrained triangulation to preserve topology
evidence_signal: The method is validated on standard multiphase benchmarks and a challenging 16-phase Rayleigh-Taylor case, matching references while keeping a very low node count.
watch_item: Robustness may depend on careful implementation of the node-empty disk and remeshing logic, especially for highly dynamic interfaces and complex phase interactions.
paper_title: Topology-Preserving Mesh Adaptation for Sharp-Interface Multiphase PFEM
